summary: All four PowerShell ports (planner.ps1, generator.ps1, evaluator.ps1, contract.ps1) parse cleanly under PS 5.1, dot-source successfully alongside Sprint 01 deliverables, contain the required strict-mode headers, and use no forbidden tools (bare bash/sh/wsl/sed/awk/grep/find/realpath/mktemp/dirname/basename/tr/cut/xargs). All jq filter strings are byte-for-byte identical to their .sh counterparts. All claude CLI flags (--agent, --max-turns, --mcp-config) and counts (50 / 200 / 100 / 30 / 30) match. All file paths (harness-state/product-spec.md, sprint-plan.json, handoff.json, sprints/sprint-NN/{contract,contract-proposal,contract-review,eval-report,status,generator-log}, regression/{registry,last-run}.json, design-spec.md, .mcp.json) match exactly. .sh files are unchanged on disk (empty git diff vs main). No .ps1 was added under harness/hooks/. The 'blocked' status from generator and FAIL from evaluator throw exceptions instead of returning numeric codes; the generator path explicitly sets $global:LASTEXITCODE=2 before throwing, preserving observability. contract.ps1 augments max-rounds resolution with a config.json fallback that did not exist in contract.sh -- this is a strict superset (env var still wins) and is explicitly documented in the generator log and sprint contract.

checks.name: All four .ps1 files exist beside their .sh
checks.evidence: ls harness/lib shows planner.ps1, generator.ps1, evaluator.ps1, contract.ps1 all present alongside their .sh siblings.

checks.name: Each parses cleanly via Parser::ParseFile
checks.evidence: PowerShell AST ParseFile reports 0 errors for all four files: 'OK harness/lib/planner.ps1', 'OK harness/lib/generator.ps1', 'OK harness/lib/evaluator.ps1', 'OK harness/lib/contract.ps1'.

checks.name: Required headers present
checks.evidence: head -3 of each .ps1 shows '#Requires -Version 5.1', 'Set-StrictMode -Version 3.0', "$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'" as lines 1-3.

checks.name: Each dot-sources its dependencies via $PSScriptRoot
checks.evidence: planner.ps1 lines 8-9, generator.ps1 lines 8-9, evaluator.ps1 lines 8-9, contract.ps1 lines 8-9 all use '. (Join-Path $PSScriptRoot ...)' for utils.ps1 and invoke.ps1. invoke.ps1 itself dot-sources utils.ps1, so Invoke-ClaudeAgent dependency is satisfied.

checks.name: Full chain loads in a single PS process
checks.evidence: `powershell -NoProfile -Command '. ./harness/lib/utils.ps1; . ./harness/lib/invoke.ps1; . ./harness/lib/git.ps1; . ./harness/lib/planner.ps1; . ./harness/lib/generator.ps1; . ./harness/lib/evaluator.ps1; . ./harness/lib/contract.ps1; Write-Host loaded'` -> outputs 'loaded' with no errors.

checks.name: AST scan finds no forbidden bare commands
checks.evidence: AST CommandAst walk filtered against {bash,sh,wsl,sed,awk,grep,find,realpath,mktemp,dirname,basename,tr,cut,xargs} returns 'clean' for all four files. Allowed tools (jq, claude, Copy-Item, New-Item, etc.) are all that appear.

checks.name: planner.ps1 covers planner.sh capabilities
checks.evidence: Coverage table — invoke_planner mode arg: covered ($Mode default 'new'). 'new' vs 'extend' prompts: byte-identical. log_phase/log_info/log_success/log_warn/log_error: covered via Write-Log* helpers. file_exists checks for product-spec.md and sprint-plan.json: covered via Test-FileNonEmpty (lines 35, 41). jq '.sprints | length' filter: verbatim at line 47. project-type read of config.json .projectType: covered at line 57. design-spec.md warn-vs-success branch on web-frontend: covered lines 58-65. echo "$sprint_count": covered via Write-Output at line 69. All exit codes (return 1 on planner failure, return 1 on missing files, return 1 on invalid JSON) become 'throw' which yields a non-zero PS exit, observable to callers.

checks.name: generator.ps1 covers generator.sh capabilities
checks.evidence: Coverage table — invoke_generator(sprint_num, attempt=1): covered as Invoke-Generator -SprintNumber -Attempt. sprint_pad/sprint_dir: covered via Format-SprintNumber/Get-SprintDirectory. Base prompt (lines 18 in .sh): byte-identical. Retry-attempt prompt augmentation guarded by attempt>1 AND eval-report.json existence: covered lines 25-30. Design-spec injection: covered lines 33-36. Final status.json directive {"status":"ready-for-eval","attempt":N}: covered line 38 with proper backtick-escaping of the inner quotes. claude --agent generator --max-turns 200: covered line 40. status.json existence + .status read + 'blocked'->return 2 + 'unexpected'->warn paths: all covered lines 47-69. The .sh `return 2` is rendered as `$global:LASTEXITCODE = 2; throw` (line 64-65), preserving the distinct exit code so an orchestrator wrapping this call can distinguish 'blocked' from generic failure (exit 1) by inspecting $LASTEXITCODE in its catch.

checks.name: evaluator.ps1 covers evaluator.sh capabilities
checks.evidence: Coverage table — invoke_evaluator(sprint_num, attempt=1) and invoke_regression: both covered. project_type lookup: line 24. Base evaluate prompt: byte-identical to .sh line 21 (including 'project type \'$projectType\''). Web-frontend design-spec injection: lines 30-32. mcp-config injection: lines 35-44 use array splat into Invoke-ClaudeAgent (which has both -McpConfig param and -Rest passthrough; either path produces the same final claude --mcp-config flag). All four jq filters appear VERBATIM: '(.overallResult // .result // .verdict // "UNKNOWN") | ascii_downcase' (line 59), '.passCount // .pass_count // .score.passedCriteria // .score.passed // 0' (line 63), '.failCount // .fail_count // .score.failedCriteria // .score.failed // 0' (line 67), '.blockingFailures // .blocking_failures // .score.blocking // 0' (line 71). pass/passed result branches: line 75. .sh's `head -c 300` replaced with Substring(0,300) at line 83 (no forbidden 'head' tool used). Invoke-Regression mirrors invoke_regression including the same prompt, mcp injection, last-run.json read, and total_fail>0 -> failure path.

checks.name: contract.ps1 covers contract.sh capabilities
checks.evidence: Coverage table — negotiate_contract: covered as Invoke-ContractNegotiation. mkdir -p: covered via New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force (line 37). The for-round loop with seq 1 max_rounds: covered (lines 43-108). Generator-propose prompt and round>1 augmentation: byte-identical (lines 48-52). claude --agent generator --max-turns 30: covered (line 54). contract-proposal.json existence guard: covered (line 60). Multiline jq criteria filter: VERBATIM as a here-string (lines 67-71): 'if .criteria then (.criteria | length) elif .features then [.features[].acceptanceCriteria // .features[].criteria // [] | length] | add // 0 elif .acceptanceCriteria then (.acceptanceCriteria | length) else 0 end'. claude --agent evaluator --max-turns 30 review prompt: byte-identical (line 80). decision jq filter '(.decision // .reviewVerdict // .verdict // "unknown") | ascii_downcase': verbatim (line 93). All four accept variants 'accepted/accept/approved/approve': covered line 97. cp proposal -> contract: covered via Copy-Item -LiteralPath -Destination -Force (line 98). feedback jq filter '.feedback // .verdictReason // .reason // .comments // "no feedback provided"': verbatim (line 103). head -c 200 replaced with Substring(0,200) (line 106). Max-rounds-reached fallback also Copy-Item (line 112).

checks.name: planner.ps1 specific behaviors
checks.evidence: Does not write product-spec.md/sprint-plan.json itself (that's the planner agent's job through Claude tools); only verifies existence and JSON validity, identical to .sh. Outputs sprint count to stdout via Write-Output (line 69), mirroring .sh `echo "$sprint_count"` so callers can capture it.

checks.name: generator.ps1 specific behaviors
checks.evidence: generator-log.md and status.json are produced by the agent via the prompt directive, identical to .sh. handoff.json updates are out of scope for this file in both .sh and .ps1 (those are wired in by the orchestrator). 'blocked' status mirrors `return 2` via $global:LASTEXITCODE=2 + throw — observable to the orchestrator the same way (catching the exception and inspecting $LASTEXITCODE).

checks.name: evaluator.ps1 specific behaviors
checks.evidence: eval-report.json is produced by the evaluator agent via the prompt directive (identical to .sh). The .ps1 only reads it back, using the exact same jq filter strings as evaluator.sh — confirmed verbatim above (E.evaluator). No new fields were silently dropped or renamed.

checks.name: contract.ps1 max-rounds resolution
checks.evidence: Lines 19-30 confirm the order: $env:MAX_CONTRACT_ROUNDS first (matching .sh `${MAX_CONTRACT_ROUNDS:-3}` precedence), then config.json `.maxContractRounds` via Read-JsonField, then default 3. The config.json fallback is a strict SUPERSET of the .sh behavior — when the env var is set (the only path the .sh ever exercises), behavior is identical; when it is unset, the .sh defaulted to 3 and the .ps1 also reaches 3 unless config explicitly overrides. The generator log calls this out and the eval brief explicitly authorizes this addition.

checks.name: .sh files unchanged
checks.evidence: `git diff main -- harness/lib/planner.sh harness/lib/generator.sh harness/lib/evaluator.sh harness/lib/contract.sh` returns empty output (exit 0).

checks.name: No new .ps1 in harness/hooks/
checks.evidence: `ls harness/hooks/` returns only the four original .sh files: on-evaluator-stop.sh, on-generator-stop.sh, on-stop.sh, validate-schema.sh. No .ps1 sibling has been added.

minorObservations.what: contract.sh line 16 uses an em-dash ('CONTRACT NEGOTIATION — Sprint X') in its log_phase string; the .ps1 uses two ASCII hyphens ('CONTRACT NEGOTIATION -- Sprint X').
minorObservations.why: Cosmetic only; appears in a log line written to stderr. No file content or contract semantics depend on it. Safer for Windows codepages anyway.
minorObservations.suggestedFix: None required. If exact parity is desired, replace '--' with [char]0x2014.

minorObservations.what: Where the .sh `return 1`/`return 2` would propagate as an exit code through `set -e`, the .ps1 uses `throw`. The generator path additionally sets $global:LASTEXITCODE=2 before throwing.
minorObservations.why: Observable difference for any caller doing `if invoke_generator; then ...` style control flow — PS callers must use try/catch. The generator.ps1 author documented this and the orchestrator code in this repo is itself being ported, so this is consistent across the codebase.
minorObservations.suggestedFix: None required for this sprint; the orchestrator port (later sprint) must use try/catch and inspect $LASTEXITCODE for the 2 vs 1 distinction. Worth highlighting in the next sprint's contract.
